title: CARB Releases Tier 2 LCFS Pathways for Crimson Renewable Energy LLC
content: The California Air Resources Board (CARB) has released six new Tier 2 Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS) pathways for public comment, submitted by Crimson Renewable Energy LLC. These pathways (Application No. B0921) cover the production of biodiesel at the company's Bakersfield facility using a variety of feedstocks. The specific inputs include North American-sourced Distillers' Corn Oil, Animal Fat, and three distinct categories of Used Cooking Oil (UCO) (Zero Energy, Low Energy, and Standard Rendering). Additionally, one pathway covers UCO sourced from Mexico. All pathways utilize natural gas and grid electricity for production, with final fuel distribution within California via truck and rail. The 14-day public comment period is currently open and will conclude on February 13, 2026, at 5:00 PM (PDT).
author: Analysts Account
